title: Customize thumbnails for video posts (including Reels)
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 600 600 Customize thumbnails for video posts (including Reels) Why can't I customize the thumb nail for my Facebook and TikTok posts? Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · 5 days ago i would 100% need to add a custom thumbnail for the video posts. now i schedule them in buffer and after posting i have to go to every plattform and add it there S Someone · 6 days ago The ability to upload a custom image for a thumbnail for Instagram and TikTok (as opposed to just choosing a frame from the video) would be HUGE. S Someone · 14 days ago Not sure why this isn’t already a feature. S Someone · 19 days ago Was just wondering if it allowed for this and its sad that they dont. I think it is needed when doing reels! S Someone · about 1 month ago This is 100% needed for Linkedin! S Someone · about 1 month ago I’m currently looking into choosing a platform to post all my social media content. Buffer seems great but unfortunately lacks this feature. Thumbnail on instagram reels is so important to keep up an identity … It seems crazy that you don’t have this feature, especially when I see that the whole buffer system feels really good quality ! S Someone · about 2 months ago I said goodbye to Unum, because it is not trustworthy in posting and gets stuck too often. But in Unum I could upload a custom thumbnail for my instagram reels. Why is it only possible to choose from the reel itsself? Feels very amateur. S Someone · 2 months ago This application is missing one most important feature and that is uploading custom thumbnails. If it wants to be better than its competitors this is a MUST HAVE feature. Thumbnails are arguably one of the most important features of a post. S Someone · 2 months ago Please consider this @Buffer 👍 S Someone · 2 months ago We’ve recently subscribed to Buffer as our primary tool for managing and scheduling our LinkedIn page. We post a high volume of video content and often need to use custom thumbnails. However, it appears this feature isn’t currently supported in Buffer. This has created a challenge for us. Ideally, we’d like to manage all scheduling through Buffer, but we’re now having to split tasks between Buffer and LinkedIn, which defeats the purpose of our subscription. title: Aggregated post analytics across all channels
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 379 379 Aggregated post analytics across all channels Is there any way to get an aggregated report by social channel that aggregates all of our individual channels' analytics into one report? So instead of manually having to add up the data in the attached report, I can just get a dataset for all channels? Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion A Admin updated status to Beta · 7 days ago I’m excited to share that our brand-new analytics experience in Buffer, called Insights, is now in Beta! Insights is a completely rebuilt analytics experience. See how all your channels are performing in one view, dive into detailed metrics for any individual channel, and get AI-powered Takeaways that tell you what content is working and why. Supports Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, X, Threads, TikTok, Bluesky, Mastodon, Pinterest and YouTube. To access the Beta, visit the Beta Features page within your Account settings. If you’ve enrolled in our Beta program already, you can click below to check it out! Explore Insights We will be regularly refining and adding new features to Insights, so please don't hesitate to share your feedback ! S Someone · Merged comment · about 2 months ago Thanks! I’d love the analytics because Blue Sky is our main social media channel. S Someone · 27 days ago good S Someone · Merged comment · about 2 months ago So you already have this info in the Sent tab, and on each individual post: why can't you just put it on the dashboard? Tech nerd wondering what the technical hurdle is! S Someone · Merged comment · 2 months ago agreed. Youtube is important. Especially for best time to post. S Someone · 3 months ago Same…I hope what ever team has this in progress gets it to the Beta testing soon. S Someone · 28 days ago +1 to this would want to see all of my social media data in on place asap S Someone · Merged comment · 3 months ago This is great because most of my work comes from my personal account not my brand page. Fingers crossed that someone will make this workable. A Admin · Merged comment · 3 months ago Hi everyone! We are gearing up to overhaul Buffer’s analytics offering in 2026, including a new experience that is simpler for navigating analytics & finding insights, but also extends support to most of the channels we support - including LinkedIn personal profiles. So, while we will not be building support for Linkedin profile metrics into Analyze itself, we’ll be building a new set of analytics features (inspired by Analyze) for which LinkedIn personal profiles will be supported. More info to come, stay tuned! S Someone · Merged comment · 3 months ago I’d love this feature because I have to manage multiple personal LinkedIn accounts alongside our brand account. I essentially treat those personal accounts as other brand accounts so I’d like to be able to view analytics in the same way. A Admin updated status to Planned · Merged comment · 4 months ago Hi everyone! I’m excited to share that in 2026 we’re planning a significant investment into analytics and insights features across all Buffer-supported channels, including Bluesky. We are envisioning building a set of new features around analytics, while also refreshing the approach for metrics you see in Analyze today. Much more to come soon. S Someone · 4 months ago I’m very surprised this hasn’t been a priority. And it was posted 2 years ago. What social media channel wouldn’t want analytics of all their channels they have connected and distributing content through Buffer? I hope this gets prioritized soon. Data driven decisions is vital. title: Automatically reuse a past Post / recurring Posts
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 373 373 Automatically reuse a past Post / recurring Posts It would be nice if you could make posts automatically return. Some kind of recycle function. But in such a way that you can already indicate this when preparing the post. There is a 'share page' button for posts that have already appeared, but every now and then I create a whole series of posts and make sure that 1 or more posts are automatically placed at the back of the queue when they have appeared. That way you can ensure that a post is always shown, even if you haven't created a new one for a while. Then you can automatically reuse the old one. Is this possible? I know Postplanner has this feature as a standard. There is another use case for text social networks too, the idea of a recurring repost/retweet of a past post. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion A Admin updated status to Beta · 13 days ago Now in Beta: You can set up your posts to recur automatically! For any posts on your schedule, you can have that post recur every n days, weeks or months up to a chosen maximum number of recurrences. Learn more about how post recurrence works in our early access guide . S Someone · about 2 months ago This is great! Thank you. This will save me a lot of time each year. A Admin updated status to In Progress · about 2 months ago I am excited to share that we have started work on a recurring posts feature! We are starting with a simple use case: to be able to recur scheduled post on a daily, weekly or monthly interval. The first iteration will have some limitations: You will only be able to recur posts on single social channels at a time. For instance, if you have a post that you’d like to recur on 3 channels, you will need to set up a separate recurrence for each of the 3 channels. Setting up a recurrence requires you to schedule the post first , then add the recurrence. We’ll be supporting recurrence directly within the Buffer post composer in a later update. We’ve imposed some (light) limits on the recurrence options, primarily to avoid infinite recurrences and spam on the social platforms. Below is a screenshot of the recurrence setup screen, to give you an idea. 😄 We are nearly ready to share this early version of the feature to a small group of interested users willing to share feedback. If you are interested, please respond to this short survey and we’ll be in touch with more information soon! S Someone · 6 months ago For me, this would be the top priority request. Reading through the comments, I may have to investigate the competition to see how they handle this. While Buffer has been most excellent, sometimes I simply do not have time to manually go through and refresh my queue. Having a recurring pool of posts to maintain activity automatically would be greatly appreciated. S Someone · 7 months ago ❤️ 1 It would be very useful if posts could be set to automatically repeat on a schedule, such as “every Monday at 10 AM.” This would save time and simplify planning for recurring content. S Someone · 9 months ago Back before I nuked my Twitter account there was a free retweeting tool for artists that would find locate your art tweets (non-reply image tweets) and then regularly retweet them on a set schedule. This was great for engagement, since I could set it to retweet so evening folks would see what I posted in the morning etc. Social media is ephemeral by nature and while it’s nice to be able to queue up a batch of reposts it would be better if Buffer could regularly boost my old posts back into people’s timelines. I’d absolutely upgrade to a paid account if this feature was offered on bluesky or the fediverse. S Someone · 9 months ago I would love this too. It would be helpful to me if this could also include scheduling multiple recurring Stories (not just feed posts). This would save me a ton of time! S Someone · 10 months ago I would LOVE to have a recurring feature. I schedule posts that repeat in 6 month intervals and having to click the calendar through to schedule each and every one is annoying. Can we get this one going? S Someone · 10 months ago I would LOVE to have a recurring feature. I schedule posts that repeat in 6 month intervals and having to click the calendar through to schedule each and every one is annoying. Can we get this one going? S Someone · 9 months ago Yes!! I wholeheartedly agree!! S Someone · 10 months ago Need something where I can schedule posts a certain number of days out (30 days, 60 days, 90 days, 120 days) from the original post. S Someone · 9 months ago Yes!! I wholeheartedly agree!! Loading content, please wait... Loading content, please wait... Loading content, please wait... Loading content, please wait... title: Automatically resize and crop large images & videos per network
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 254 254 Automatically resize and crop large images & videos per network Would like to post large images without reducing their file size. A feature where Buffer automatically resizes the image to be below the max allowed by the social media sites would be much appreciated. Short-form video platforms (Instagram, TikTok) seem to be even stricter about this. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · 29 days ago Yes. This is a much needed feature. S Someone · 7 months ago This is such an important feature to have. Not having it currently adds the most time to scheduling. Other platforms like Later have this feature built in. S Someone · about 2 months ago 100% agree with this, it’s such a pain having to resize images for instagram 1 by 1! S Someone · 9 months ago In the same vein, Buffer currently severly compresses LinkedIn video way more than is needed, down to low quality 720p when LinkedIn supports 4k video up to 5GB in size, so taking that into account, video shouldn’t be so aggressively changed for LinkedIn. S Someone · 12 months ago Yes. In this particular instance, the Instagram API has a hard limit of 300MB for reels and 100MB for stories. Currently Buffer limits IG Reel posting to 100MB which seems like a fault of Buffer. Why not just limit up to 300mb for reels? S Someone · over 1 year ago This would be much appreciated S Someone · over 1 year ago Would love to see this one S Someone · over 1 year ago 👍 1 Please fix the API so that it will auto-resize. At the very least, add some kind of trigger when I initially set a post that tells me it will be rejected so I can plan accordingly. I don’t get a “post fail” until it’s actually scheduled and the time is set, and then I’m scrambling to post it natively. This completely defeats the purpose of Buffer. S Someone · over 1 year ago Thanks S Someone · almost 2 years ago I think this will cost in server time, so it may need to be a paid-tier feature only, or pushed to a client side script. But it is very frustrating that some auto sizing and cropping cannot be done. Better would be a subject identification ‘AI’ that allowed cropping to be done to a clear subject in the image. Also worth testing what the platforms already do when you push through oversized/misshaped images, some of them do automatic adjustments already, perhaps not nice ones though. Insta seems that most picky, twitter and Linkedin often make good guesses. A Admin · almost 2 years ago Hey folks, we’re working on a way to have an auto-crop / resize capability for media. I’ll update this space with more information soon! Loading content, please wait... Loading content, please wait... Loading content, please wait... Loading content, please wait... title: Plan/Schedule Ideas on the Calendar
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 227 227 Plan/Schedule Ideas on the Calendar I would like the ability to place ideas on a calendar for planning out a month instead of having to maintain an external content planning calendar. Being able to see ideas in the same calendar view as posts would also be nice so I can see where there are ideas without planned posts. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion Pinned M Manager · over 2 years ago 👍 2 Hey all! Nathan here — product manager at Buffer! Just wanted to drop a note that we are actively working on improving Ideas for content planning, so I love to see this feedback. Currently, we’re working towards implementing more kanban-like features into the Ideas space itself, which hopefully helps with the content planning process. After we implement that, we’ll be looking into how we can better bridge the gap between Ideas and the calendar, so the feedback here is very valuable. Please continue to share any additional ideas or thoughts in the comments! And, if you’re open to chatting more directly about how you do content planning, I’d love to set up a call! Reach out to me at nathan @ buffer.com ! S Someone · 6 months ago Hi Nathan, is there a rough timeline for this being completed? thanks! A Admin · 6 months ago 👍 1 Hi there! Apologies for the slow response here. We expecting to support adding ideas to the Calendar early next year, as part of a larger set of changes to improve the composing and planning experience across Buffer. We’ll have more details to share soon :) S Someone · 3 months ago We really need this! Is there an ETA release date on this feature yet? :) S Someone · 4 months ago Essential for turning Buffer into a proper Content Calendar. At the moment I can save ideas as drafts assigned to a particular date and time, but they appear in the calendar simply as ‘draft’ rather than with a title showing. S Someone · 6 months ago Social Pilot is the model for this. They allow you to see both Ideas (in the form of Notes) and scheduled Posts on the calendar for planning. The thing that could be improved upon is being able to assign tentative publishing dates to Drafts so that they would also populate on the calendar. That would allow you to manage everything from the calendar interface. It would essentially eliminate the need for an external calendar as many have said. S Someone · 7 months ago Really looking forward to being able to put ideas on the calendar!! that would make buffer my all-in-one tool S Someone · 8 months ago To map out ideas on the calendar would be a game changer: being able to, within the calendar convert them directly from ideas to drafts and then scheduled directly. (or switch them back to an idea if plans change) At the moment, we are using the ideas section as as a totally independent area. This means that once we decide to we like an idea we have to create a draft post just to be able to plan out the month, then we have to build it but: The title from the idea hasn’t carried across to the draft post The original idea is still cluttering the ideas space and either has to be deleted manually or be manually tagged or moved to an alternate group S Someone · about 1 year ago What is the progress on the calendar update to integrate planning? S Someone · about 1 year ago 👍 1 I’d love to see this feature along with an integration to Asana. I am responsible for actually creating posts in Buffer and pushing them out to our channels, but I work with a bunch of project managers that provide content for posts about their projects and it would be awesome to have a way to integrate a content calendar in Buffer with Asana tasks so I don’t have to maintain two separate calendars to gather content from the project managers. S Someone · over 1 year ago Leaving a +1 to this suggestion! This is what 99% of scheduling tools lack (I think Plot is the only one that does this, and they’re quite new). Placing ideas onto a calendar view would reduce a ton of friction for those who need to plan more than a week of content in advance. Most marketers (or business owners with products who market seasonally, for example) need to plug in high-level reminders of what’s coming before actually sitting down to schedule it. S Someone · over 1 year ago 👍 1 This is the exact feature I’m looking for. If you implement it, please include the ability to use tags. I use an Excel spreadsheet to plan out my content, but I would definitely prefer to keep it all on one platform. Loading content, please wait... Loading content, please wait... title: Notes or blocks on the calendar
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 209 209 Notes or blocks on the calendar Be able to put a note on a day on the calendar. This could either be just for a simple reminder, or block that day from scheduling (for example in the case of a holiday where I do not want to post). I want to be able to look at the calendar and know a day I planned a post (like a holiday or a launch day). I don’t want to have to create the post, just know I am supposed to. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · about 2 months ago ETA on this? Its so simple…. S Someone · 2 months ago Totally agree with this. Badly needed! S Someone · 3 months ago We need this!! Is there an ETA on this? S Someone · 6 months ago Definitely being able to block off specific days on the calendar would be huge. I don’t necessarily want to post something on every holiday or have content as regularly planned but I end up having to manually move every post so that it doesn’t keep trying to post on that holiday. S Someone · 7 months ago we need this. it’s essential for planning S Someone · 9 months ago I really want to be able to drop my weekend events into the calendar so I know which upcoming events to plug without having to flip between 2 calendars. S Someone · 9 months ago Good idea! S Someone · 9 months ago This feature is important enough that I’m currently considering moving away from Buffer to find an option that includes it. As it stands, I’m having to coordinate several calendars with ideas and events, rather than being able to record directly in Buffer when something is coming up that I will need to post about. S Someone · 8 months ago Did you find a calendar that allows notes? I need one also S Someone · about 2 months ago I am currently debating between Buffer and HeyOrca which does allow you to put events on the calendar and even import your country’s national calendar. But Buffer has the analytics at a lower price point… S Someone · 10 months ago Yes please! 100% need this! When looking for a new social media planner, this is the one thing that is stopping me moving our account over to Buffer. S Someone · 10 months ago I think this is a good idea as well, but let me throw in a twist. I see this being helpful to mark special events on the calendar…as a way to remember that it is more than just a regular day. As such, we maintain a “Special Events” calendar in Google Calendar. It would be great if I could connect the posting calendar to my Special Events calendar to show those special events. This way I don’t have to remember to transfer everything over. Some of the special events would be the company anniversary, trade shows, volunteer events, holidays, and I’m sure many others. Loading content, please wait... Loading content, please wait... title: Distinct workspaces for clients and brands
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 168 168 Distinct workspaces for clients and brands I'd like to be able to have multiple workspaces for my different brands. I currently manage socials for 19 brands under one parent organization, so 72 social accounts. It would be easier to manage if you were able to create separate workspaces to group them by. Similar to how you can toggle between organizations. Business details: Physical store or location (e.g. retail store, cafe, or tourist attraction), More than 500 employees Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion Pinned A Admin · about 1 year ago Hey folks, I would love to learn a bit more about how you would expect this feature to work alongside billing. Would you prefer billing be done at an account level, or would you expect that each workspace would have a way to be billed separately? This is one of the key problems we’re working through right now, so any insights on how you’d like to manage this would be very helpful! S Someone · about 1 year ago 👍 2 Account level is working for us. We pass on the applicable costs to clients. More “granular” billing is not a feature we need at this time, but I’m sure it would be helpful to some. S Someone · about 1 year ago For our use case, we’re happy for billing to still be at the account level, but I can definitely see workspace billing (or workspace-grouped invoicing) being useful for marketing agencies. S Someone · about 1 year ago Agency owner here. I don’t mind to deal this on account level. I don’t bill my customers for my service uses… S Someone · about 1 year ago We already pay for each account? So let me guess… the ability to restrict access to content i.e. a workspace would be an additional fee? You know what…Do your thing, I’m about to close my account soon enough. S Someone · about 1 year ago Most certainly at an account level. The channel nesting is for a more organized UI, that’s about it. In my mind, rather than listing out each individual social media account on the left-hand side as it currently stands, you’d simply be able to group them together on the left-hand side as you see fit and tap an arrow to expand each group. I suppose a decent UI example would be from our task management software. We have a few many groups, but then as you see, I hover my mouse over one of them and I can tap an arrow to expand it, and everything in that group, or nest, is then revealed. That’s all I was simply hoping for with Buffer so that such a lengthy list of accounts is no longer needed, and organization would be improved and simplified by grouping social channels by each client. Hope this makes more sense now! Nesting example 3.jpg 120 KB Nesting example 1.jpg 89 KB Nesting example 2.jpg 90 KB A Admin · about 1 year ago @jb2creative - we’re not planning on making this an additional fee or add-on for workspaces, but we’re just trying to gauge how our users would expect this to work (i.e. each workspace having a different plan and billing method set-up). Appreciate your feedback on this nonetheless! A Admin · about 1 year ago @Matt @callum.corrigan @ozancaglargil @accounts - really appreciate your input with this! We’re trying to map this out at the moment and this is one of the larger open questions we had. It’s good to have such strong validation and feedback that account level is where you’d expect billing and plan selection to be at. S Someone · about 1 year ago ❤️ 2 Billing at the account level would be acceptable for me as well as many have stated. A middle ground might be offering a simple price breakdown somewhere in the account/channel selection per “workspace.” E.g if I have 5/20 channels in workspace A and my total bill is $200, it’d be nice to easily know I’m paying $50/month for this workspace/client. Just two cents. Love you’re currently on this. Thanks! S Someone · about 1 year ago 👍 1 ❤️ 2 Great! And just to be clear, my feature suggestion is different from switching into different workspaces for each brand; it’s simply being able to nest groups of social media accounts together so that all brands/clients can be more organized in one area. But when I submitted this feature request, they lumped it into this idea. Hope this makes sense! S Someone · about 1 year ago 👍 2 Hi, Billing is less an issue, and should be postponed, no need to take care of it now. The most important feature is Grouping the Social media Channels under a Workspace (Or Folder) and then allocating the Social media channels to the workspace. Right now having all the channels in the Side bar as one long list is hard to manage. S Someone · 20 days ago We want workspaces!! Guys! I love Buffer, I love the new UI, but having distinct workspaces where people can connect their social channels themselves is very important to us. This self-serve infrastructure is REALLY critical and would save a lot of time! I am sure a lot of agencies and brands would like this. This would easily put you ahead of competitors that already offer workspace capabilities. plz provide updates on when we can expect this feature to be available. Thank you so much! S Someone · about 2 months ago S Someone · 3 months ago I would LOVE this as well. The channel groups are great when setting up a post, but I would love to be able to view the calendar by a group, versus hunting through the whole list and only checking the brands and channels by division that I work on. S Someone · 6 months ago 👍 1 I run a small agency with several clients, and one of my biggest challenges is keeping client accounts completely separate when managing multiple social profiles. A feature that would really elevate Buffer is the ability to create distinct workspaces — one per client — where: Each workspace has its own set of social media accounts (to avoid cross-posting across clients). Each workspace can be billed separately , making it easier to pass costs directly to clients without manual tracking. Permissions, reporting, and settings are tied to the workspace, giving each client their own “container” inside Buffer. Why this matters Other social media management tools like Sprout Social and Hootsuite already offer multi-workspace or organization switching features. These are useful for agencies, but the downside is their pricing is significantly higher , especially as you scale with multiple clients. Buffer has always stood out for its affordability, ease of use, and clean interface . By implementing workspace-level organization and billing , Buffer could offer the same premium functionality as Sprout Social, Hootsuite, or Agorapulse , but at a much more accessible price point. That would make Buffer the best choice for agencies and small businesses who want enterprise-level features without enterprise-level costs. Additional advantages of workspaces in Buffer Separation & safety : No more risk of cross-posting content to the wrong client. Scalable growth : Onboarding a new client is as simple as spinning up a new workspace with its own profiles, reports, and billing. Flexible billing : Option to bill all workspaces under one account (for simplicity) or bill workspaces separately (ideal for agencies). Client transparency : Ability to invite clients into their own workspace for collaboration, approvals, and reporting — without exposing other clients’ content. Custom settings per workspace : Each client could have their own time zone, posting schedule, and brand preferences. Why this would make Buffer the market leader If Buffer implements multi-client workspaces with flexible billing while keeping its current competitive pricing structure , it would position itself as the go-to social media management platform for agencies, freelancers, and businesses. No other tool would match the combination of: Affordability Ease of use Advanced agency-level features For small agencies like mine, this would eliminate the need to consider higher-priced platforms and make Buffer the clear winner among social media scheduling tools . S Someone · 7 months ago Can channel groups be shared team wide? A Admin · 7 months ago Hey @d - at the moment, they’re only applied to the individual user. We will consider this for when we make broader improvements for how we handle team accounts :) S Someone · 6 months ago Got it. Would you consider rolling this out before other team account changes? S Someone · 9 months ago Good idea! S Someone · 9 months ago I am a freelance social media manager, and have three separate clients, all with separate social media accounts. I want to be able to use all of Buffer’s tools for each brand, but keep the process of content planning and approval separate for each brand. I essentially want one master account to be able to manage and hop in and out of multiple brands’ dashboards, workspaces, etc. A Admin updated status to Planned · 9 months ago Hi everyone! I’ve moved this specific feature request back into the Planned status; we shipped a lightweight Channel Groups feature to more easily batch-select channels when posting, but it’s far from the true “client workspace” that many of our users have also requested. This will reflect the latter request moving forward. Sorry for any confusion here! A Admin updated status to Released · 9 months ago Available now on all paid plans! Loading content, please wait... title: Schedule and Publish via API
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 124 124 Schedule and Publish via API It would be great if Buffer had an API that enabled posts to be submitted/scheduled/published to configured social media sites programmatically, so that we could integrate Buffer functionality with other systems. Just the same features that already exist, but controlled through an API. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion A Admin updated status to Beta · about 2 months ago This has now been released for users who are part of our beta track. To get access, go to https://publish.buffer.com/settings/beta and turn on Buffer API. From there, you can navigate to both the API page and Integrations page in your settings . S Someone · 3 months ago looking forward, let me know. S Someone · 4 months ago Looking forward for the API Access! S Someone · 5 months ago I believe the APIs are needed ASAP, releasing a minimal subset even just to beta tester would be the best way for you to get feedback early on. Official MCP server and other things mentioned in this thread are cool, but can come at a later moment. Right now access to Buffer API is limited to make and other automation websites that severely limit flexibility on what can be accomplished. S Someone · 2 months ago I agree. Something like what Sanity.io or Kit.com provide would be awesome. Might give Buffer more “stickiness” S Someone · 5 months ago Of course. We are waiting for. S Someone · 7 months ago Why is there no MCP server or API for N8N???? Why still talking about Make and Zapier that’s like 3 years ago… is Buffer going out of business, should i find another social platform manager, I’m worried A Admin · 7 months ago Hey @erik - we are actively working on having an MCP server and being n8n compatible with the release of our new API. We will share more updates here when we have them. A Admin · 10 months ago 🎉 1 Hey folks, If you’re keen to get early access to our beta API, please register your interest here: https://buffer.com/developer-api We’re looking forward to sharing more very soon :) S Someone · 10 months ago 👍 1 an api is a must in 2025, MCP as well, n8n can do webhooks, https requests and automation is the key ! Zapier and Make are great, but API features constantly lag behind your product features hence would love access to a direct Buffer API. A Buffer MCP server would also be interesting to consider in future given the “vibe marketing” trend. A Admin · 10 months ago Hi @stuart - thanks for the feedback! We will definitely be focused on building an MCP server as part of our MVP for beta. We’re also prioritizing this with a dedicated team, so our hope is that this is an ongoing project and not a one-off. We’re committed to ensuring the long-term viability of our API and really building a flexible and extensible platform. We recognize that we didn’t prioritize this appropriately with our previous API, and we’re course correcting for our new version. We’re looking forward to getting this into the hands of developers soon! title: Support Bluesky content labels
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 71 71 Support Bluesky content labels What Bluesky has a feature to self-moderate/self-label content that you post to the platform. Followers usually expect you to label this content properly as sometimes the automated Bluesky label system is slow or is (often) incorrect. Why Some content, especially if you are in the art or the publishing sector (think romance book covers), may fall under artistic nudity or be rather suggestive. Bluesky has the feature of allowing for self-labeling this content, and it’s heavily encouraged that you self report. This allows followers to filter out content they don’t want to see, and prevents those that are not of age from seeing it either. This means that you are able to maintain your audience without alienating them, a great way for growth while also promoting the various works of content that you’re able to do. How Posts can be made to bluesky with a bit of extra json added to the post metadata, an example of what this code looks like can be found here . It’s not much more code, and would only require an extra tiny dropdown in the UI to select from (currently) 4 global options, of which they are found here . As of writing, these tags are: porn ←this is the adult tag sexual ← this is actually the suggestive tag, despite its terrible internal name nudity ← for artistic nudity graphic-media ← gore, blood, violence Normally, on bsky, this feature looks like a button that appears when attaching an image. Upon clicking the button, this pop up appears: It would be great if it could be supported, as a publisher of material that is often spicy in nature, having something that let’s me schedule these posts ahead of time and still respect the bsky ecosystem would honestly drive me to purchase a plan. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · 9 months ago I honestly don’t understand how this is not implemented yet. It feels like such an important feature to protect both people who don’t wanna see adult content and people who do want to see it. Sadly, Buffer is useless to me without this feature. I’ll keep looking for an alternative. S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago Their AI is good at labeling most content, but it would be a lot better if I could do it myself from Buffer. If they miss things (which they’ve done) it could get my account into trouble. It’s an important safeguard for both the content creator and the consumers of content. S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago Buffer is useless to me without this feature! S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago This is so so important for me. My audience is awake during my sleeping hours. I need to be able to tag the posts so I’m not sharing NSFW content with people who can’t view it. S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago need this, required not for adult only but suggestive too this is a critical issue directly related to ban at bluesky and to avoid ban users MUST label their post cannot post safe without this S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago I’ve been using Buffer for months now (with a different account) and this absolutely was a feature when I first started. I find it absolutely bizarre that it’s subsequently been removed. Community moderation is a fundamental part of how BlueSky works, and using the NSFW and other labels allows us to respect the community that doesn’t want to see or engage with NSFW content. But we can’t do that and use Buffer to schedule posts with that feature now removed. I was about to switch to the paid version of Buffer because it’s an incredible tool, but if I can’t label my content appropriately when using Buffer to schedule posts, there’s not much I can really use it for and I’m not willing to pay towards it. Especially since this was an included feature, but has subsequently been removed for apparently no good reason. S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago This is so weird. Buffer doesn’t allow adult content to be send. You account will be disabled if you do. S Someone · Merged comment · over 1 year ago 🚀 1 Not having this feature added asap will be a deal breaker for my business. I will absolutely have to use an alternative. Buffer is shooting themselves in the foot by not having this already while the entire adult industry is jumping ship from X. You are losing money every single day. S Someone · Merged comment · over 1 year ago 👍 1 Really hope this is being worked on as it’s needed desperately. S Someone · Merged comment · over 1 year ago 👍 1 This feature is definitely needed. title: Bulk Upload Media Files to a Media Library
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 60 60 Bulk Upload Media Files to a Media Library Allow users to upload multiple image or video files at once to Buffer for easy posting and editing. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion M Manager · 7 months ago Hi all! I wanted to drop a note here regarding bulk upload. It’s seems there are two camps of requests here: uploads via CSV and uploading multiple media files to a library. CSV Upload If you’ve been asking for this feature, I’m happy to say that V1 has been released! While not every field for every channel is supported, we plan to keep extending functionality to this feature. You can learn more about that here . Bulk Upload Media to a Library Currently, Buffer does not have a dedicated media library. However, this is a feature we’ve discussed frequently and is one we’d likely implement in the future. If you’re looking for this, please keep commenting and upvoting this topic! As we already support creating multiple posts via CSV, I’m going to edit the topic of this post to focus more specifically on the media library use case. If that is what you’re looking for, please keep commenting here! S Someone · 5 months ago I’m uploading 10 videos a day on 4 accounts. Bulk upload straight from my PC would save so much time for me because its so exhausting to post 1 video at time. S Someone · 5 months ago yes! title: Tag-Based Scheduling for Efficient Queue Management
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 55 55 Tag-Based Scheduling for Efficient Queue Management It would be awesome if posting schedules could be tied to tags. For example, let’s say I tag posts as “Quote Testimonial” or “Video.” I’d love to set it up so anything with the “Quote Testimonial” tag goes out on Tuesdays and Thursdays, and anything with the “Video” tag goes out on Mondays and Wednesdays. That way, when I hit “add to queue,” Buffer would know the best slot to place it in based on the tag, instead of me having to move it around manually. This would make it super easy to keep different types of content consistent without having to think about it every time. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · 17 days ago This would be a game-changer for my workflow. I run a music education channel and post 7 pieces of content per week: 3-5 Reels (tutorials from my YouTube videos), 1 performance Reel, 1 interactive Reel, and 2 Carousels. Reels perform best for reach and should go out in the evening, while Carousels drive more engagement and work better mid-week. Right now I have to manually manage the order in my queue to make sure each content type lands on the right day — which defeats the purpose of having a scheduling tool. Tag-based queues would let me set it and forget it: Reels go to their schedule, Carousels go to theirs — automatically. This is the one feature that would make Buffer truly complete for content creators with a structured weekly strategy. S Someone · 4 months ago YES!!! This would be such a great feature to have, would make life so much easier! M Manager · Merged comment · 7 months ago Hi there! Thanks for the suggestion! I’d love to get some more clarity on what you’re looking for. Would this be to schedule all drafts of a specific tag at once? Or would this be the ability to designate a tag on a queue slot, so that every time you schedule a post with the tag “dog” it goes to a specific time slot? S Someone · Merged comment · 4 months ago Thanks for the follow up! Happy to clarify. What I’m looking for is the ability to have multiple independent queues, each with its own publishing schedule. For example: I have two recurring content types. Type A posts should always publish on Mondays. Type B posts should always publish on Fridays. Right now, because there’s only one queue, I have to carefully alternate posts A, B, A, B to make sure they land on the correct days. What would help is having separate queues, such as: Queue A publishing every Monday Queue B publishing every Friday Then I could add posts to the appropriate queue in bulk, without worrying about order. Each queue would handle its own timing. S Someone · Merged comment · 9 months ago ❤️ 1 I am currently looking at switching from SocialBee to Buffer, but absolutely need to be able to schedule specific categories (tags) of content to go on certain days/times. This has been a great feature for me in SocialBee and I need to be able to continue using it if I do move away! S Someone · Merged comment · 10 months ago hey, yes indeed I would very much appreciate this kind of feature because this way I can set up that advertisement content is posted on afternoons only etc. S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago yes. this is a must. we need an instagram story channel, and an instagram post channel. totally separate approaches, so combining is near impossible. the second you hit shuffle, obviously everything goes wrong. S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago Hypefury does this. Expected Buffer to do the same. S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago Would love to types of content slots in the queue. Like have education spots, promotion spots, random thought spots. So when you queue, you could tell it send it to the next random thought spot, or the next education spot. That way batching all promotion content for the month gets spaced out over the month - not all together. And the random thought posts still go in between value content. S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago I bought Buffer annual thinking that’s what the tags did. Would love this so we can quickly schedule the right things at the right time without worrying about messing up. I often have a few ideas based on one topic, but want these spread out over time. It’s a real pain to have to keep a mental run of “do one this Tuesday, one next Tuesday”. Means I have to try and do all my scheduling at once. title: Bulk Editing of Posts
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 42 42 Bulk Editing of Posts If I need to move several Posts at one time, I currently have to do them one at a time. So imagine have to move 6 different channels (Posts) that are on the calendar for a week and I need to move all of them to the following week. Further imagine if I need to move an entire “series” of posts (i.e. I have 10 weeks of postings, over 5 days each week on 6 different channels) down one week. Creating a “Open” week before a series begins. Currently I’d have to move each post one at a time, week by week. Working with Posts as a “Batch” as well as Indivdually is critical for those of us using Buffer and posting dozens of posts at a time over longer periods. (which I assume is the whole point of using a tool like Buffer) Please look into this let those of us who posts a lot of posts, frequently know when you can update this process. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. title: Inject post into queue
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 41 41 Inject post into queue If I want to “inject” a post into a queue, I have to drag a post and replace an existing one. Then the one I replaced get’s moved to the back of the queue. If I have many posts, this becomes extremely tedious. It would be great to drag it in, then have everything after “shift” up to make room. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago I’m only looking at one channel (linkedin) - and so I’m not so bothered about moving posts between channels. But I really want to be able to change the order / insert new posts in particular places . Quite often I write 4 or 5 posts on a subject, and I want them to go out every third or fourth post (for variety for the readers) so I want to put them in between say posts 2 and 3, then between posts 6 and 7, then between 10 and 11, etc. Just to be able to drag them and drop them in between some of the existing posts in the queue would be brilliant. If I have to do it by swapping pairs of posts it’s really difficult and slow, and it messes up the existing order. S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago ❤️ 1 This is super hard and needed. Right now if you are working on 3 channels, adding posts, and imagine you forget one in one channel, you lose the overview as one is added to the bottom. Horrible to align the content in different channels and check each post one by one. A Admin · Merged comment · about 1 year ago Hey @info - thanks for this feedback. This is super helpful, and along the lines of some interactions we are looking to address around managing the queue. I am wondering - are you using the All Channels view to visualize your queue across your multiple channels? We do have some limitations in that view, but I’m wondering if drag-and-drop and some easier ways of duplicating posts over to a “missed” channel could address some of that concern you raised. S Someone · Merged comment · about 2 years ago This is a very important feature to address. I need a visual overview S Someone · Merged comment · about 2 years ago yes! it’s bad enough to drop a post to the one you want to swap it with, but more serious is that you can’t drag a post to go in-between two existing posts, e.g. put it in after number 3, and the current number 4 then slides down to number 5, etc. And yet this is the thing I want more than anything else. In fact I’m planning to leave buffer because I can’t do it. S Someone · Merged comment · over 2 years ago I just wanted to add that it could work like Google Calendar where I can click on a post, then use the mousewheel to scroll around, then when I release the button, it drops it. S Someone · Merged comment · about 2 years ago Calendar would be good too, but harder for you to do - I just want to be able to move the queue around, and at the moment I can’t easily do that. title: Support for language localization / options
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 39 39 Support for language localization / options I am currently working for a French company and most of the employees are not fluent in English. Since the platform is quite complex for non-English speakers, I was wondering if you could add "French" as one of the supported languages? Thanks. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · 4 months ago Dear Buffer Team, I'm a loyal user from Japan. I've introduced Buffer to everyone across multiple social networks (X, Bluesky, Threads), and now Buffer is gaining significant attention among Japanese users. However—many Japanese users aren't fluent in English. If possible, we'd be thrilled if you could add Japanese language options! Please consider it! S Someone · 11 months ago Dear Buffer Team, First of all, I’d like to say congratulations on such a great app! Buffer is truly impressive, and it offers many helpful features — especially for a free tool. It has been incredibly useful for organizing my social media content, and I really appreciate how intuitive and efficient the platform is. The only suggestion I have is regarding the language options. It would be amazing if you could add a Portuguese translation . Many users in Brazil (and other Portuguese-speaking countries) struggle with English, and having the app available in Portuguese would greatly improve accessibility and the overall user experience. Thank you for your hard work and for providing such a valuable tool. Keep up the great work! Best regards, Mariany Lelis > > > > > > > Prezada Equipe do Buffer, Em primeiro lugar, gostaria de parabenizá-lo por um aplicativo tão incrível! O Buffer é realmente impressionante e oferece muitos recursos úteis — especialmente para uma ferramenta gratuita. Ele tem sido incrivelmente útil para organizar meu conteúdo nas redes sociais e eu realmente aprecio a intuitividade e eficiência da plataforma. A única sugestão que tenho é em relação às opções de idioma. Seria incrível se vocês pudessem adicionar uma tradução para o português. Muitos usuários no Brasil (e em outros países de língua portuguesa) têm dificuldades com o inglês, e ter o aplicativo disponível em português melhoraria muito a acessibilidade e a experiência geral do usuário. Agradeço pelo seu trabalho árduo e por fornecer uma ferramenta tão valiosa. Continuem com o ótimo trabalho! Atenciosamente, Mariany Lelis A Admin updated status to Someday · about 1 year ago We don’t have a specific timeframe in mind for supporting other languages in the Buffer product, but this is part of our long-term vision. I’ll provide an update here once we have clarity as to when/how we’ll approach this. Thanks! S Someone · over 1 year ago Request for Spanish language support Hi! I’d like to echo this request for additional language support. I currently work in an environment where many users are Spanish speakers and not fluent in English. This can make it challenging to navigate and fully understand a complex platform like this one. It would be amazing if you could consider adding Spanish as one of the supported languages, as it’s one of the most widely spoken languages globally. Thank you for your attention and for considering this improvement! 😊 S Someone · over 1 year ago It could be really usefull for my company as well, as i’m the only english speaker and i’m leaving soon… S Someone · about 2 years ago Please :) A Admin · Merged comment · over 2 years ago Obrigado pelo seu pedido! Neste momento, estamos explorando a melhor maneira de suportar outros idiomas no Buffer, incluindo o português. Não temos um plano ou cronograma firme, mas continue assistindo ao nosso quadro de sugestões para atualizações! S Someone · over 2 years ago To complete the thread @Brandon (Product Manager @ Buffer) : I am french user (but no issue with english myself). I work with clients to whom I would like to recommend buffer but the language barrier is a bit hard for some small businesses. A proxy is to use google translate (or deepl which can be more accurate) but again not ideal as some clients are not super digital geeks. Besides, even if the new AI assistant is handling french, there are some bugs once you kickstart the conversation, see the video attached in that link https://www.swisstransfer.com/d/1dae0981-fb9e-493e-b413-b7fe51005d91 eventually in wordpress some plugin exists to fully translate a website, like polylang i think, might be worth having a look if such plugins can be added to buffer , howeever this might slow down the UX… https://polylang.pro/ A Admin · over 2 years ago Thank you for your reply @Someone ! This is very helpful context for assessing the right approach to handle language localization in the short vs. long term. We’ll absolutely keep this in mind. S Someone · over 2 years ago Hi there! Just saying - if there's a need to translate to PT-BR, let me know and I’ll be happy to help with that. My team would love to use Buffer, but for now we can’t. We simply don’t use a social media management platform because of the language barrier. Best regards, G. Ribeiro A Admin · over 2 years ago Hi there @Someone ! Thank you for your note. I wanted to ask - how does your team handle language barriers on other product? Do you use any third-party tools to help translate products that otherwise don’t translate natively to Portuguese (eg. Google Translate)? Or do you simply prefer not to use tools at all without language localization? S Someone · over 2 years ago All of the products we currently use are localized to PT-BR. There are some very few exceptions where we need to use stuff in english, but those cases are usually redirected for me to deal with. That being said, I consider the Google Translate UX to be a huge mess, even when integrated within the browser. It works fine to read articles and blogs, but on dynamic apps and pages where multiple languages are being used, it is completely unusable. A Admin · over 2 years ago Thank you for the reply @Someone and for your candid feedback on Google Translate. Will definitely keep this in mind! A Admin · over 2 years ago Hi there - I’m part of Buffer’s product team! I’m responsible for several aspects of Buffer’s product experience, and I’m interested to learn more about your needs for supporting other languages. We are evaluating how best to support language localization in Buffer, and the amount of effort (and time) it would take to support this might depend on our approach. I was wondering: What products or tools do you (and your teams) use to help with language gaps? Do folks use tools like Google Translate, Grammarly, or other tools in their workflow? How effective are they at solving challenges with language? Where do they have shortcomings? What products do you (and your teams) use that have really great language support? Thank you so much! If translation was needed, we would probably use Deepl or Google Trad. They are quite good and work well both ways. But we don’t want to go through them to use a software. It’ll be better to have French directly set up. At the moment, we can’t afford to waste time on translation, so we only use tools that have embedded translation and/or French directly available during the setup. An example could be Midjourney for AI image generation. A Admin · over 2 years ago Thank you @Someone - this is extremely helpful! Loading content, please wait... Loading content, please wait... title: Make it easier to manage Hashtags
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 38 38 Make it easier to manage Hashtags Make it possible to simply manage your hashtags outside of the workflow of creating/planning content. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · 5 months ago Also, make the hash tag manager be available in the cross-platform new composing dialog and when composing ideas. I’m using the same hash tags for all platforms. Right now, it is only accessible once you customize for each platform, which defeats the purpose. M Manager · 5 months ago Thanks for the feedback @canopi.travel ! We’ve recently added the hashtag manager to the multi-channel composing dialog too, so you can apply hashtags before you customize for each platform :) S Someone · about 1 year ago It is hard to see, add, group, edit and utilize hashtags in that tiny window with the tiny scrollbar next to the workflow window. The scrollbar handle is a dot and is hard to use. The program continually thinks I am trying to exit and asks me over and over if I want to save and exit when I am working on hashtags. The window that contains the actual hashtags is so small that you can only see a few hashtags from each category at once. If you want to see what each set contains, it is an endeavor to scroll through and try to remember what you just saw. If you could glance easily at the set without going into edit mode it would be so much better. My posts require custom groupings of hashtags, as each post is a different type of art. I don’t have a standard set of hashtags to just plug in each time. The way the hashtag manager is set up it is almost impossible to select some relevant hashtags from each set. If I tried to make a set for each possible type of post I would require dozens of sets to memorize, that I can’t glance at to see what they contain. This makes the hashtag manager not much better than a text edit or spread sheet workaround. The photo attached shows a set that contains 30 hashtags that I often use. How does one remember which 30 hashtags are contained in this set when only four show? Inserting the entire set then deleting the ones I don’t want to use is not a timesaver. S Someone · about 1 year ago Yes a popup does fly out that shows the hashtags but you can’t select them or copy paste them, so if you want to use some hashtags from each category you have to click the three dots, click “edit group”, click the dot in the scrollbar then scroll, highlight the hashtag you want to use, click copy, go to your post and click paste, then do that for each one you want to use from a different set. That is six or seven clicks to add a hashtag, harder then just recalling and typing. S Someone · over 2 years ago I am moving from Tailwind. They have an amazing Hashtag Manager. It would show me in colors what Hashtags are big and which Hashtags I can use that are rather niche. A Admin · over 2 years ago Hi @Someone ! Thanks for your note. I do see that you are on one of our paid plans, which grants you access to the Hashtag Manager tool. Can you let me know if you are having trouble using it, or have feedback on it? Thanks! S Someone · over 2 years ago 👍 1 Hi Brandon. I had my first opportunity to see what Hashtag Manager is all about when I used it a few days ago when I went to post 2 items over the weekend to Instagram and Facebook, and to create hashtags for my other channels. However, after doing some reading and research on the use of hashtags, I wanted to add a few more to my Instagram and Facebook channels and couldn’t find any mechanism to do that. In other words, unless I am creating/scheduling, there’s no place to “work on” or really “manage” one’s hashtags lists — either adding or removing — so that they are ready to go when it comes time to create a post. And, in my opinion, there definitely should be. A Admin · over 2 years ago Thanks @Someone - this is a great point. I like the idea of having a place to manage/curate hashtags that you can then apply to your posts when creating. I’ll note this in the suggestion board and we can follow up when we explore this idea for the roadmap! S Someone · over 2 years ago 👍 1 Great. The Hashtag Manager was a big plus for me in upgrading. I wanted to get rid of my excel spreadsheet that I have been using for hashtag management. So, I'll look forward, hopefully, at some point to having an option to curate at Buffer. Thanks for the quick response. title: Send a schedule of content for client review
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 34 34 Send a schedule of content for client review We need to get client’s approval for a month’s worth of content scheduled. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · 6 months ago I need this feature — today S Someone · almost 2 years ago This is so critical, so that clients are not having to sign into an account or our account, some sort of approval portal would be great to be able to fold into our process. S Someone · about 2 years ago It would be great to be able to publish a page and send the client a link to it. I currently use Notion that way, and it would be so nice to do it in Buffer to save that extra, time-consuming step. Second choice would be a pdf or something like that. Anything that lets my client see it easily without having to create an account, which I know they won’t do in reality. S Someone · over 2 years ago Yes! title: Allow team Owners to also request approval for posts they create
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 30 30 Allow team Owners to also request approval for posts they create As head of marketing I write the content (and am an admin), the other users also have admin rights. I can’t create posts that they can approve (due to being admin and not being able to use the approve feature). The only option they have is to schedule them / add to queue. The option to have them approved will assist with technical sign off / owner approval of information in the post. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · about 1 month ago Same. Please add this feature. S Someone · about 1 month ago Yes, this is a very obvious, necessary feature. It is very common in smaller organisations for the content writer to be the admin and need the approval of management/colleges to post. I figured that a feature like that would be a standard option with a post-management application. Please add this feature S Someone · about 2 months ago I would also like this feature, to be able to give someone access the admin centre but set our own approval flows. S Someone · over 1 year ago I would also appreciate this feature to make it possible to request approval from another user, but not require it on every post. Along with that, an option for the approving user to simply mark it “approved” in some way instead of having to send it to the queue or schedule it would be important. In my role, I’d like my boss to review some posts, but she does not need to approve everything. And since we want some posts to go out on certain days, I don’t want her to have to spend time ordering the queue correctly. Thank you! S Someone · almost 2 years ago Hi there! Did the update happen already? I am having the same issue as well and I was considering using Buffer, but because this feature doesn’t exist, I am thinking of another option now. I am a freelancer and to manage multiple accounts and as Owner role. A Admin · almost 2 years ago Hi @Someone - thanks for your message. At this time, we do not support this feature where account Owners can also request approval from others on their team - however we are evaluating it as a future roadmap item. I will up-vote this feature request on your behalf, if that’s okay! A Admin · over 2 years ago Hi @Someone - thanks for your note! I checked your account and it does appear you have each of your team members set up with full posting access. If not, you can change these permissions such that you can require the users on your team to request approval for any posts they create. Have you tried this? If not, here is a help center article that can help walk you through this! If this is not meeting your needs around posting approval, let me know - I’d love to learn some more specifics around what you’re looking for around approval and permissions. Thanks again! S Someone · over 2 years ago Hi Brandon thanks for yours. Yes I tried that. The issue is as I set up my account first as admin and then added the other team members (Managing & commercial director) I need them to sign off on the content due to quality processes. As I am unable to edit my user account there is no option to filter out or request for them to approve my content without sending them emails / and vice versa. It would be helpful to have the option for teams to send which posts they are waiting sign off by one or more people (no matter the user account type). Thanks A Admin · over 2 years ago Ah - Thank you for your reply, this makes a ton of sense! title: Auto Retry Failing Posts
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 29 29 Auto Retry Failing Posts Retry failing posts automatically I would like to offer a different perspective to those people who have "posts [that are] very time-sensitive [and] would rather the post not be published than be published late". As you know, I use Buffer because of time-zone differences. We are a global company and most of our customers are awake while we sleep. Our posts are less time-sensitive than the above, but still somewhat time sensitive in that we would prefer them to go out on the same day as planned, and not 12 hours later on the following day when we humans wake up again and can press the manual retry button. While the above users may only want to retry for no more than 2 minutes (guessing), we would prefer Buffer to keep retrying for, say, two hours. Perhaps not on a tight busy loop, but say, every 5 minutes. You say, "there's no way for us to know which posts are time-sensitive", but I suspect that actually, it's not posts which are time sensitive, but buffer accounts that either post in a time-sensitive way or do not post in that way. Thus, a buffer account could have a setting which would determine for how long buffer will keep retrying a transitory failure (no point in retrying an absolute show-stopper of course). You could even do clever things like change the retry interval based on the length of the total retry time. E.g. if retry is 2 minutes, try every 15 seconds; but if retry is 2 hours, try every 15 minutes. Or you could just ask the user for two numbers. The default value of this setting when introduced could maintain the current behaviour. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · about 1 month ago I’m also a user who has less time sensitive posts. It’s fine if it’s 12 or 24 hours delayed. For me an account or channel level setting would be fine to mark it as not time sensitive. To me getting the post out is much more important than it being posted at a specific time. S Someone · 8 months ago 1) If one post fail, try next scheduled post on the same time A Admin · over 2 years ago Hi @Someone - thanks so much for the suggestion! We’re looking into how we can improve post reliability (and decrease post failure) across the board in the coming weeks, so we will definitely consider this as an additional improvement. title: Allow post approval notifications to be assigned to specific admins
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 25 25 Allow post approval notifications to be assigned to specific admins We use Buffer with a team structure where contributors (like interns or junior staff) submit posts for approval. Currently, all admins receive a notification when a contributor submits a draft—even if only one person is responsible for reviewing them. We'd love the ability to assign a specific admin as the approver for each contributor or channel, so that only the intended person gets notified. This would help reduce unnecessary pings, clarify workflows, and streamline approval accountability. Right now, our other admins are getting alerts they don’t need, which causes confusion. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · 7 months ago Please!!! You should be able to select who you notify, maybe this can be based on access levels and who is assigned to each channel? S Someone · Merged comment · 11 months ago Cannot express how important this is for my workflows. The current way approvals work is essentially useless to us. S Someone · Merged comment · about 1 year ago Being able to send a post for approval from my teammates even though I am an admin is important. We need to get eyes on every post just to double check for mistakes before publishing. S Someone · Merged comment · almost 2 years ago This would be huge improvement for us — right now, we're juggling both Trello and Buffer for content approval. Our current process is Team members draft content in Trello for initial review. Once approved in Trello, they move it to Buffer as a draft. I then check Buffer drafts, ensure everything's set up correctly (mentions, tags, etc.), and move approved posts to the queue. This workaround is clunky (no notifications for posts to review, no way to distinguish between in-progress drafts & those awaiting approval) but Buffer's "Approval Required" user setting is too rigid for our needs. While most posts need my sign-off, there are exceptions — like live event updates or time-sensitive content when I'm unavailable. An improved approval option would save us time and help reduce the risk of errors slipping through 🫶 Subscribe to post Get email updates when post changes Get notified Status Planned Board Feature Suggestions Posted by S Someone Assigned to A Admin Posted on 10 months ago Upvoters 25 S S +23

title: Custom channels via Webhooks
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 20 20 Custom channels via Webhooks Add your own channel via API. Create a webhook so that I can get a scheduled update pinged to an API. From there I can add it to a website, a CMS, a social media platform that isn't supported (yet), or an automation that runs on a post. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · over 1 year ago definitely worth it to keep things tidy and complete in one place. S Someone · over 1 year ago This feature is literally the one that would convert me to Buffer. I’m Murlok.io ‘s creator and I built my own posting system to publish on the website and Discord at the same time. Currently contemplating doing the part to also post in Thread and X. This would allow me to have buffer as a entry point and have my news posted everywhere it matters to me (my website first). S Someone · over 1 year ago Adding a full-fletched Buffer API may not be a strict requirement here (although it would be nice to have). By configuring a shared secret when setting the webhook url, the payload could be fully embedded and encrypted on the webhook request. A Admin · Merged comment · over 2 years ago Hi there @Someone ! Thanks for your suggestion. We are exploring how to support other channels and forums outside of the major social platforms - since there are millions of forums around the internet, we are exploring ways to support these different forums as generically as possible, such as with an API or notification-based approach. I’m going to categorize your suggestion under our topic of “custom channels”, which we mean to represent any other place you’d want to publish content that isn’t a major platform. Let me know if this works for you, and if you have any additional thoughts or questions! A Admin · over 2 years ago ❤️ 1 Hi @Someone - thanks so much for the suggestion! We’re actually investigating this opportunity at the moment so will keep this space updated! title: Increase character limit for Mastodon
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 19 19 Increase character limit for Mastodon Hi! Do you know that - Mastodon provides 1000 symbols per post. Not 500. But Buffer provides only 500 for Mastodon. Fix it, please! Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion S Someone · 9 months ago The Mastodon software’s default character limit is 500, with some exceptions and nuances in how that is counted. (URLs, for example, get counted as 23 characters , regardless of length.) But many instances have raised their limits to support longer posts. I use an instance that supports 1,024 characters. So I am used to posting content >500 characters, and Buffer’s cap on 500 means I can’t use Buffer to schedule such posts. I appreciate that Buffer shows a tally of # of characters, and highlights in red anything exceeding that amount. I’m assuming this accounts for Mastodon’s nuances in counting (though I have not tested this!). Assuming Buffer already accounts for this… I would suggest some possible resolutions: Check the character limit on the specific instance in use, then use whatever that number is instead of a hard-coded 500 (this only works if supported by the Mastodon API), or Allow scheduling posts with a character count of >500, but provide lots of warnings in the UI, knowing that there may be errors at post time (if not supported by the instance), and/or Allow the user to manually set a target character limit (e.g., 1,024), so Buffer only shows red text and prevents posting when the user-set limit is exceeded S Someone · about 1 year ago It would be great to default to 500, but allow the user to change the post size. I haven’t seen a Mastodon server with such a low content window, and this makes it difficult to cross-post great content to Mastodon and also to e.g. LinkedIn. S Someone · over 1 year ago this makes buffer absolutely useless. buffers reason to exist is to act as a proxy - that´s the only thing - so what is the product worth, if you fail to do the one and only thing the product got created for ? it´s insane !!! S Someone · over 1 year ago I work on a variety of mastodon servers, all with different character limits. Is there a means of getting the server character limit upon authentication? A Admin · over 2 years ago Hi @Someone - thanks for the suggestion. We are supporting the out of the box, standard limits from Mastodon. Every server is different, so we opted to support the ones that were applicable to the majority of servers. We will keep this space updated as we explore server specific limits. S Someone · about 2 years ago Since most Mastodon servers I know now have the 1000 character limit have you revisited this? title: Batch-edit posting schedules
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 18 18 Batch-edit posting schedules The ability to edit the schedule across all channels at once. Comments Join the discussion and help shape the future of Buffer. Once you’re signed in, you’ll be able to: Vote on posts Share your thoughts React to comments Submit new posts Join the discussion A Admin · Merged comment · 12 months ago Hi @hello ! Thanks for your suggestion! We’re actually exploring some improvements to how scheduling in Buffer works now, so I was happy to see your request to set times for individual channels for a post. I was curious how you thought this might work – do you see yourself wanting to specify a unique posting time for each instance of a multi-channel post, or would you prefer to set “presets” for each channel to follow? I ask, because the latter option is similar to how our Queue functionality works today, though we’re looking to improve it to be a bit more intuitive. On the former option, I am curious if you’d want to do this for every post you schedule, or something you’d use occasionally and for specific kinds of posts. Would generally love to chat more about this if you are willing! Thanks again! title: A plan between the free plan and unlimited posts.
body: Buffer Sign in with Buffer Feature Suggestions Roadmap What's New 15 15 A plan between the free plan and unlimited posts. Hi. I want to make a suggestion about your plans. It would be really great if there was something between the free plan and the essentials plan. I don't need unlimited posts. I probably only need about 10 posts but on all of the channels. It seems ridiculous to pay for something unlimited when I don't need that much or to be limited to the free option and only have three channels. Maybe you could do some thing in the same price range of the essentials where there is limited posts, but you can access all the channels.
